title: Virtualized, auto-scrolling event log with LazyVStack + ScrollViewReader
source: Apple HIG (Lists and tables) + SwiftUI performance guidance on lazy stacks and scroll anchoring for streaming logs
description: A streaming roro event feed rendered in a plain VStack or List keeps all rows materialized and janks as the buffer grows. Using LazyVStack inside a ScrollView with a stable row identity plus ScrollViewReader for 'follow tail' behavior (auto-scroll only when the user is already at the bottom, pause when they scroll up) matches terminal-log UX expectations and keeps memory bounded via a ring-buffer cap.
proposed_changes: Wrap the feed in `ScrollViewReader { proxy in ScrollView { LazyVStack(spacing:0) { ForEach(events) { row.id($0.id) } } } }`. Track an `isAtBottom` flag; on new event call `proxy.scrollTo(last.id, anchor: .bottom)` only when isAtBottom is true. Cap EventStore.events to the last N (e.g. 2000) with a ring buffer so long sessions don't grow unbounded. Give each event a stable UUID id emitted from roro_events.py.
reject_reason: No files were actually modified (false success)
